---
title: "cositos"
---

**cositos** is a binding-free [anywidget](https://anywidget.dev)-inspired backend core:
define a widget front end once, drive it from **any** Jupyter kernel language.

This page proves the docs pipeline: the widget below is **rendered statically** — no live
kernel — from a serialized widget document embedded into the page by `cositos.embed`.

```{python}
# | echo: true
# | output: asis
from IPython.display import HTML

from cositos import dump_document, embed_snippet

ESM = """
export default {
  render({ model, el }) {
    const btn = document.createElement("button");
    const out = document.createElement("span");
    const paint = () => { out.textContent = " count = " + model.get("n"); };
    btn.textContent = "increment";
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => { model.set("n", model.get("n") + 1); model.save_changes(); });
    model.on("change:n", paint);
    paint();
    el.appendChild(btn); el.appendChild(out);
  }
}
"""

doc = dump_document([("counter", {"_esm": ESM, "n": 3})])
HTML(embed_snippet(doc))
```

The button is a real cositos widget: its frontend ESM ran in your browser against state
that was serialized in Python and embedded here. Interaction is client-side (no kernel).
